introSection: Tornado is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Parque de Atracciones de Madrid in Madrid, Community of Madrid, Spain. Opened on 23 May 1999, it is the second full circuit inverted coaster to be made by Intamin.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: The train exits the station, makes a right turn and climbs the chain lift hill. Riders drop to the left and head into the first vertical loop. A right banked turn leads to a second vertical loop. An another right turn is followed by a corkscrew. After a figure eight turn, riders hit the final brake run. The train makes a right turn and returns to the station.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Black track and supports. The ride used to feature yellow track and green supports.

fullText:
Tornado is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Parque de Atracciones de Madrid in Madrid, Community of Madrid, Spain. Opened on 23 May 1999, it is the second full circuit inverted coaster to be made by Intamin.



The train exits the station, makes a right turn and climbs the chain lift hill. Riders drop to the left and head into the first vertical loop. A right banked turn leads to a second vertical loop. An another right turn is followed by a corkscrew. After a figure eight turn, riders hit the final brake run. The train makes a right turn and returns to the station.

Black track and supports. The ride used to feature yellow track and green supports.
